skill.name: ancplua-docs
skill.description: Search ANcpLua ecosystem documentation for SDK features, analyzer rules, Roslyn utilities, and related configuration guidance.
skill.summary: Find answers across ANcpLua SDK, analyzer, and Roslyn utility documentation.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline paths, and documentation examples. A real medium concern remains because the skill instructs agents to read hardcoded absolute local repository paths outside the skill package.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Absolute Filesystem Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to search specific absolute /Users/ancplua repository paths. This is legitimate for a local documentation librarian, but it can cause unintended reads outside the skill package when matching paths exist.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The absolute paths are explicit and the skill allows Read, Grep, and Glob. No evidence shows network exfiltration, writes, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings come from Markdown code fences, inline file names, and suggested Grep or Glob search patterns. I found no executable script, shell command runner, or command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation and search guidance, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill metadata only permits Read, Grep, and Glob.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is a Text False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings do not identify cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain the skill description and a Markdown path example, not MD5, SHA1, or password hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language and Markdown formatting. No cryptographic API usage or hash computation appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is a Search Example False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a Markdown code fence boundary near read-only Grep examples. I found no host fingerprinting, environment collection, or persistence behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is documentation context, not a reconnaissance command. Nearby content lists topic search patterns for documentation lookup.

content.value_statement: ANcpLua users need fast answers across several related repositories. This skill maps SDK, analyzer, and Roslyn utility documentation for targeted lookup.
actual_capabilities: Routes ANcpLua questions to SDK, analyzer, or Roslyn utility documentation.
Identifies documentation files for analyzer rules AL0001 through AL0017.
Suggests Grep and Glob searches for ANcpLua topics and symbols.
Maps SDK feature areas such as polyfills, banned APIs, testing, and web defaults.
Encourages answers with source file references and related documentation links.
limitations: Requires the referenced ANcpLua repositories to exist at configured local paths.
Does not include the full documentation content inside the skill package.
Cannot verify package behavior without reading the target repositories.
Uses static documentation maps that may become outdated as repositories change.

use_cases.title: Find SDK Configuration Guidance
use_cases.description: Locate documentation for MSBuild properties, SDK variants, banned APIs, polyfills, and test project settings.

use_cases.title: Research Analyzer Rules
use_cases.description: Find rule documentation for AL0001 through AL0017 and connect each rule to related implementation guidance.

use_cases.title: Explore Utility APIs
use_cases.description: Look up DiagnosticFlow, SemanticGuard, SymbolPattern, and related helper APIs across utility documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for one ANcpLua topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the ANcpLua documentation for banned APIs and summarize the main guidance with source paths.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an Analyzer Rule
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate lookup for a specific rule.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ANcpLua docs to explain rule AL0017, when it triggers, and where related documentation lives.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Related Areas
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare how ANcpLua.NET.Sdk and ANcpLua.Roslyn.Utilities document source generator support. Include related files.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Documentation Trail
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced investigation that needs multiple references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace documentation for test utilities, fake logging, and SDK test configuration. Return a concise reading order.

output_examples.output: The skill points to the analyzer rules index and individual AL0001 through AL0017 rule pages, then summarizes the rules table.
The response identifies the Roslyn utilities documentation, explains the DiagnosticFlow topic, and lists related utility pages.
The response directs the user to SDK legacy support documentation and highlights TimeProvider, Index/Range, and nullability attribute areas.

best_practices: Ask about one ANcpLua package or feature area at a time.
Request source paths when you need traceable documentation answers.
Keep the local repository paths updated before using the skill.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a general .NET documentation source.
Do not assume answers are current when local repositories are stale.
Do not ask it to modify projects; it is designed for read-only documentation lookup.

faq.question: What documentation does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers ANcpLua.NET.Sdk, ANcpLua.Analyzers, and ANcpLua.Roslyn.Utilities documentation maps.

faq.question: Does it contain the full documentation?
faq.answer: No. It contains a map and search strategy for finding documentation in local repositories.

faq.question: Which tools does it need?
faq.answer: It is designed for read-only file lookup with Read, Grep, and Glob.

faq.question: Can it answer analyzer rule questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It maps AL0001 through AL0017 rule pages and related analyzer references.

faq.question: Can it help with Roslyn utility APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It points to pages for DiagnosticFlow, SemanticGuard, SymbolPattern, contexts, pipeline helpers, and symbol utilities.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: The referenced ANcpLua repositories should be available locally, or the paths should be adjusted before use.
